channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Bonteheuwel Shopkeeper Murder: Charges Dropped Against Four Suspects Amid Evidence Shortfalls
item.description: A spaza shop owner named Umar was murdered on 13 July in Bonteheuwel, with CCTV footage revealing a woman allegedly pretending to be a customer before a man shot him and handed over the firearm. Four ...

item.description: The South China Sea rivalry between the United States and China threatens to overshadow the Association of Southeast Asian Nations (Asean) meeting, according to recent developments. US Secretary of St...

item.title: Marcos reaffirms Philippines' commitment to peace and sovereignty
item.description: President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. emphasized the Philippines' unwavering commitment to peace, sovereignty, and national dignity during his speech at the Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP) Change of Co...

item.title: Internal strife in SAD (Punar Surjit) as leaders back Waris Punjab De, leading to Jhunda's expulsion
item.description: The article discusses a significant political development within the Shiromani Akali Dal (Punjab) faction, SAD (Punar Surjit), where several leaders have announced unconditional support for the Akali ...

item.title: Italian airline expands flights to Tanzania, boosting tourism and economic ties
item.description: Italian airline Neos has launched scheduled flights to Kilimanjaro International Airport in northern Tanzania, marking a significant expansion of air connectivity between East Africa and European mark...

item.title: Philippines Prepares for Potential Cyclone as LPA Enters PAR
item.description: A low-pressure area (LPA) is expected to enter the Philippine area of responsibility (PAR) today and may develop into a tropical cyclone within 24 hours, according to PAGASA. The system, currently loc...

item.title: Canada proceeds with Gordie Howe bridge opening amid U.S. trade tensions
item.description: The federal government has confirmed it will proceed with the opening of the Gordie Howe International Bridge on July 27, despite the absence of a cross-border ceremony with the United States. This de...

item.title: Oversight Judge Confirms Receipt of Complaints Against Idac Head
item.description: The office of the Idac oversight judge has confirmed receiving complaints against advocate Andrea Johnson, the head of the Investigating Directorate Against Corruption (Idac). This marks a departure f...

item.title: Defence Minister Denies Resignation Rumors, Promotes Security Collaboration
item.description: General Christopher Musa (rtd), Minister of Defence, has officially dismissed rumors about his alleged resignation, stating he has no intention of leaving his post. During a meeting with President Bol...

item.title: Examples of News Headlines in Spanish and English
item.description: This article provides examples of news headlines in Spanish and English, emphasizing the importance of accurate and concise summaries in media. It discusses how headlines can sometimes be clickbait, u...

item.title: Tanzanian Entrepreneur's Journey from Poverty to Motorcycle Taxi Ownership
item.description: A Tanzanian entrepreneur’s journey from extreme poverty to owning a motorcycle taxi business highlights the role of determination and microfinance in East Africa’s informal transport sector. The story...

item.title: Asset Forfeiture Ordered for Maumela Amid Tembisa Hospital Corruption Allegations
item.description: Over R326 million in luxury assets linked to businessman Hangwani Maumela have been forfeited to the state following a Gauteng High Court order, but no arrests have been made. Civil society groups lik...

item.title: Ecuadorian president faces sexual assault allegations from 14-year-old
item.description: A 14-year-old girl has accused Ecuador's president of sexual assault, sparking widespread public outrage and political controversy. The allegations, which were initially dismissed by the president's o...

item.title: ELRA and Heifer International partner to boost youth agribusiness through affordable equipment leasing
item.description: The Equipment Leasing Registration Authority (ELRA) and Heifer International Nigeria have deepened their partnership to expand affordable equipment leasing for young entrepreneurs, aiming to unlock ac...

item.title: Dr Rolf Gomes' Heart of Australia initiative transforms rural healthcare access
item.description: Dr Rolf Gomes, Queensland's 2026 Australian of the Year, has revolutionised healthcare access for remote communities through his Heart of Australia mobile clinics. By outfitting trucks with diagnostic...

item.title: Kitchener Justice of the Peace Accused of Judicial Misconduct in Colleague Interaction
item.description: A Justice of the Peace in Kitchener, Ontario, is facing allegations of judicial misconduct related to inappropriate behavior toward a colleague, according to a report by CTV News. The incident, which ...

item.title: Dharmendra Pradhan criticizes Congress for exploiting students in political protests
item.description: Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan accused Congress leader Rahul Gandhi of using students as political tools during the CJP protest over NEET exam irregularities. The protest, which saw thous...

item.title: Burnham faces challenge of boosting public optimism amid rising pessimism in UK
item.description: A new Ipsos survey reveals widespread national pessimism under Prime Minister Andy Burnham, with 83% of Britons believing the country is heading in the wrong direction—a sharp increase from 55% in 198...

item.title: Congress leaders detained after protest outside PM Modi's residence over NEET paper leak as political tensions escalate
item.description: Senior Congress leaders, including party president Mallikarjun Kharge, Rahul Gandhi and Priyanka Gandhi Vadra, staged a surprise sit-in protest outside Prime Minister Narendra Modi's residence in New ...

item.title: Chelsea signs Morgan Rogers on long-term deal
item.description: Chelsea FC has officially confirmed the signing of England international Morgan Rogers from Aston Villa. The 23-year-old forward, who has been a key player for Villa, joins the Blues on a contract unt...
